sutta: the buddha said monks there are these 5 timely gifts what 5 1 gives to the newcomer to 1 going away to the sick and food is hard to get the first fruits are filled and orchard he first sets before the virtuous monks these are the 5 timely gifts that's the end of the sukta here the buddha is talking about the gifts that a lay person can make to among the first 1 is uh to make an offering to a newcomer that means a new monk who has just come into the monastery and perhaps he might come in on an empty stomach on the way he might not have eaten something so a person who comes to the monastery regularly he might offer something to this manga in for example in the evening something that is allowable for example honey or sweets or cheese whatever is allowable in the monastery and the second 1 to 1 going away 1 going away sometimes among going away to another monastery especially in the buddha's days in the buddha's days sometimes monks sometimes they walk long distances so among going away might be going on a long [Music] long journey to another monastery and along the way sometimes it might be difficult to get food so the buddha in his wisdom sometimes made certain allowances for example if a man expected that he was about he's going to walk on a long distance and along the way there might not be people there to offer him food it was allowed by the monk by the buddha that a monk could carry food with him could bring along food with improvision so that he could sustain himself so a lay supporter could offer a monk going away some food and then to the sick the third 1 when the monk is sick a certain allowances also allowed now that is why uh for example there's certain suit it is mentioned that a devotee would bring for example clear meat soup to a sick monk in the afternoon or in the evening if the monk is sick or prepare some special food for the monk that is suitable for him because of his sickness etc and the fourth 1 when food is hard to get when there's a feminine or something and then when the monks go on arms around in the butt might be difficult to get food so either they support their nose there might bring in bring more food now and then fifth 1 the first fruits are field and orchard the first sets before the virtuous these are concerning farmers farmers when their crops bear fruit or their fields bear fruit then the first harvest they will supply to the monastery those what devoted followers of the buddha would do these are called the timely gifts
